A.evidence: Mostly faithful prose and order, but author affiliation accents/citations are noisy, some math indices are garbled such as y_t^0, and Table 1 is absent.

B.evidence: Clean prose and LaTeX equations with good hierarchy, but repeated page headers/sidebar text intrude and Table 1 is absent.

C.evidence: Prose is mostly present but has OCR errors like Kalchbrneren/Èneco and awkward math tokenization; Table 1 is missing despite reaching results.

D.evidence: Good equations and prose early, but it drops the end of page 1 introduction, injects raw bbox JSON, and omits Table 1.

E.evidence: Prose mostly follows the paper but equations are badly damaged or missing, e.g. p(y) and c_i formulas, and Table 1 is absent.

F.evidence: Contains location tokens, duplicated passages, hallucinated/reworded prose, repeated lines, missing equations, and no table.

G.evidence: Readable and mostly complete prose through shown pages, but some indices/directional arrows are wrong and Table 1 is missing.

H.evidence: Many formulas are replaced by formula-not-decoded or glyph garbage, alignment figure OCR text pollutes the end, and Table 1 is absent.
